content: Always run cargo fmt before release.
Never commit generated credentials into fixtures.
Use the USB target directory for local Cargo builds.
Verify JSON output stays parseable before closing a bead.
Check the bead dependency graph before claiming work.
Ensure pack hashes remain stable for identical inputs.
Prefer structured parsers for response envelopes.
Remember to include provenance for promoted rules.
Run focused tests before the broad readiness gate.
All context pack items must include actionable reasons.
The search response should report degraded recall honestly.
A closure note is required when a bead ships a sentinel.
Always sync beads after changing issue state.
Never hide a failed search arm behind a success envelope.
Use markdown snapshots for renderer regressions.
Verify that stdout contains only machine JSON in json mode.
Check feature flags before exercising experimental aliases.
Ensure memory IDs are quoted in shell examples.
Prefer explicit workspace flags in e2e scripts.
Remember decay jobs are opt in until documented otherwise.
Run bash syntax checks for changed shell drivers.
All degraded codes should have catalog fixtures.
Use existing core APIs for triad wrappers.
Never create custom retrieval fusion inside the CLI layer.
Check pack markdown parity after changing JSON shape.
Ensure tombstoned memories stay excluded by default.
Verify fixture counts before trusting accuracy metrics.
Use deterministic ordering for every emitted item list.
Always record the command that produced a spike artifact.
Prefer dry-run probes when measuring wrapper inference.
Required repair hints belong in every blocking error.
Run cargo fmt after editing Rust tests.
The release failed when the search index was stale.
A regression in pack rendering duplicated every item.
The build broke after enabling the optional adapter.
A JSON schema error blocked the contract test.
The migration problem appeared on the seeded workspace.
The smoke test reported a storage failure in the audit path.
The pack hash bug made identical inputs diverge.
A broken fixture hid the missing degraded code.
The parser failed on an empty markdown table row.
The link suggestion problem came from stale tag data.
The context command errored when the DB path was absent.
The status report showed a regression in freshness scoring.
The formatter failure came from a long generated line.
A search quality bug returned only zero-score hits.
The policy detector broke on a harmless token-budget note.
The e2e driver failed because the binary path was stale.
The graph refresh problem left centrality scores unavailable.
The output contract error came from mixed stdout diagnostics.
The backup roundtrip bug changed tag ordering.
The closure linter failed after a sentinel bead was mislabeled.
A stale workspace problem produced an invalid migration report.
The replay command broke when a pack record was missing.
The redaction problem removed the entire policy sentence.
The shell driver failed on a missing jq binary.
The 2026 release was tagged after the local verify pass.
The 2025 workspace was migrated with the v2 schema.
The 2026 audit run fixed the stale closure notes.
The 2024 fixture was changed during the markdown parity pass.
The 2026 harness ran the triad walkthrough twice.
The 2025 importer did preserve every memory tag.
The 2026 benchmark was recorded with the release profile.
The 2024 corpus was seeded before the search probe.
The 2026 agent changed the pack profile defaults.
The 2025 smoke suite ran on the retained workspace.
The 2026 context check did include rendered markdown.
The 2024 diagnostic run was captured in the support bundle.
The 2026 migration fixed the workspace id mapping.
The 2025 release notes were updated after the gate passed.
The 2026 replay did select the same pack items.
The 2024 search probe ran with lexical-only mode.
The 2026 handoff was written after the verification ledger.
The 2025 graph snapshot was refreshed during maintenance.
The 2026 driver changed the outcome artifact schema.
The 2024 backup was restored into a clean workspace.
The 2026 command log ran before the final sync.
The 2025 policy audit fixed the allowlist examples.
The 2026 test matrix did cover compact output.
The 2024 contract fixture was normalized after review.
Cargo target artifacts live outside the repository checkout.
The default branch name is main.
Context packs contain a rendered markdown text field.
The response envelope schema is ee.response.v1.
FrankenSQLite is the durable storage foundation.
Frankensearch provides hybrid retrieval for memories.
Asupersync is the runtime foundation for this CLI.
The mcp adapter is optional for normal operation.
Pack skipped entries describe candidates excluded from context.
Memory links store relationships between durable memory records.
The query file schema name is ee.query.v1.
The outcome artifact belongs under tests logs active.
The triad surface consists of note pack and why.
The search quality report includes a source mode field.
Workspace configuration influences context pack budgets.
Golden fixtures preserve stable machine output contracts.
Audit log rows capture durable memory mutations.
A pack hash starts with a blake3 prefix.
The support bundle contains redacted diagnostic artifacts.
The closure linter reads bead taxonomy labels.
The corpus seed script populates the reference workspace.
The graph snapshot stores derived centrality features.
The verification ledger links commands to closure evidence.
The status command reports subsystem readiness.
